title: Tanacetum scopulorum
intro: Tanacetum scopulorum, also known as Mountain Tansy, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and nervous system disorders, due to its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and anticonvulsant agent. The bioactive constituents of Mountain Tansy include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Mountain Tansy is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Tanacetum scopulorum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Tanacetum, a group of flowering plants often referred to as tansy. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Tanacetum scopulorum shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants, including daisy flowers and a similar growth habit. Within the genus Tanacetum, Tanacetum scopulorum is often distinguished by its unique characteristics, such as its specific range or growth patterns. Further classification may include its species within the larger plant kingdom, such as kingdom Plantae.
uses: Tanacetum scopulorum, also known as rocky tansy, is a plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Tanacetum scopulorum for medicinal purposes. In some parts of the world, the plant is also used as a natural insect repellent.

constituents: Tanacetum scopulorum is known for its active constituents, which include sesquiterpene lactones such as scullarin and scopoletin. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and are commonly found in the plant's leaves and flowers. The sesquiterpene lactones present in Tanacetum scopulorum have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In addition to sesquiterpene lactones, the plant may also contain other bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
